red_state.summary: Focused test command compiled and executed. The suite failed only on missing US3 recommended phase skill implementation surfaces.

WorkflowRuntimeService > startPhase snapshots active phase recommended skill resolutions for resume-stable prompts

workflowToolPolicy > recommended phase skills do not bypass workflow tool permissions or enable SkillTool globally

validation_output.stdout_summary: Ran 203 tests across 6 files: 197 pass, 6 fail, 1107 expect() calls. Failures assert missing phaseSkillSnapshots/recommendedSkillStatus/recommendedSkills prompt and policy surfaces. Existing compact carryover bounded evidence test passed.

concerns: Project cognition query returned readiness=blocked with stale dirty baseline, so live task packet, contract, quickstart, and test/service reads were used as authoritative evidence.

Session API test assumes the builtin agent-development discussion phase will expose sp-discussion as an active-phase recommended skill once US3 implementation lands; adjust only if the canonical builtin skill name differs.
recovery_hints: Persist phaseSkillSnapshots when starting workflow sessions/phases, using phase skills from the template and resolver results rather than recomputing prompt meaning on resume.
Render a distinct Active phase recommended skills prompt block from the persisted snapshot, including available and unavailable/degraded groups, and keep scheduled SkillTool calls empty.
Add recommendedSkillStatus to workflow metadata/summary with total, available, unavailable, degraded, evidenceCount, and bounded activePhaseItems.

Include final report recommendedSkills snapshots and evidence, filtering to used, relevant-skipped, and relevant-unavailable outcomes and excluding irrelevant unused checklist noise or skill file contents.
Update workflow tool policy guidance so recommended phase skills are explicitly advisory and do not enable SkillTool, model, effort, fork, shell, hook, or unrelated tool permissions.
